research.product: Kubernetes API server (core v1 Pod log subresource)
research.interface: GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}/log (stable core v1); researched against v1.36

official_sources.title: Kubernetes v1.36 official OpenAPI specification (kubernetes/kubernetes, release-1.36)
official_sources.used_for: the readCoreV1NamespacedPodLog operation: its exact query parameters (container, follow, insecureSkipTLSVerifyBackend, limitBytes, pretty, previous, sinceSeconds, stream, tailLines, timestamps) with their documented semantics — including 'timestamps adds an RFC3339 or RFC3339Nano timestamp at the beginning of every line' — its text/plain string 200 response, and the Status definition fields

official_sources.title: Pod | Kubernetes (kubernetes-api reference)
official_sources.used_for: confirmation that the log read is a core v1 Pod subresource at GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}/log and that pod lists live under /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods

official_sources.title: Kubernetes API Concepts
official_sources.used_for: the meta/v1 Status error envelope returned for API failures (kind Status, status Failure, message, reason, code) and chunked streaming behavior of long-running requests

research.language_rationale: The official Java client (kubernetes-client/java) wraps exactly this REST surface, but the harness forbids third-party jars; the log subresource is plain HTTP text streaming, and java.net.http.HttpClient with an InputStream body handler is the idiomatic zero-dependency way for a JVM log collector to consume it — the byte-level UTF-8 line reassembly is the point of the exercise.

verified_facts: Pod logs are read from the core v1 subresource GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}/log; a 200 response is a plain string (text/plain), streamed chunked when following.

The operation's query parameters include container (which container; defaults to the only container), follow (boolean, default false), tailLines (integer, lines from the end), and timestamps (boolean, default false); sinceSeconds, previous, limitBytes, insecureSkipTLSVerifyBackend, pretty and stream also exist but are unused here.
timestamps=true adds an RFC3339 or RFC3339Nano timestamp at the beginning of every line of log output — fractional seconds vary, so chronological ordering requires parsing, not string comparison.
tailLines counts from the end of the log; when tailLines is specified the newer stream parameter may only be unset or 'All'.

Non-2xx API responses carry a JSON meta/v1 Status body (kind Status, status Failure, message, reason, code) even for the text log endpoint — e.g. 404 NotFound for a missing pod and 400 BadRequest for an invalid container.
Pods are listed at GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods with labelSelector filtering.
